guest_info.name: Daniel Schmachtenberger
guest_info.title: Founding member of the Consilience Project

summary: Daniel Schmachtenberger discusses the factors that could lead to civilizational collapse, the nature of exponential technologies, and the importance of developing social technologies to guide and bind physical technologies. He also explores the role of individual and collective action in steering civilization away from self-destruction.

main_entities.name: Consilience Project
main_entities.context: Organization aimed at improving public sensemaking and dialogue.

main_entities.name: Self-terminating systems
main_entities.context: Systems that depend on a substrate they are debasing, leading to their own termination.

main_entities.name: Exponential technology
main_entities.context: Rapidly advancing technologies that can pose existential risks if not properly managed.

main_entities.name: Social technologies
main_entities.context: Technologies that guide, bind, and direct physical technologies for long-term survival.

main_entities.name: Mimetic theory
main_entities.context: Theory of desire based on imitation, leading to conflict.

main_entities.name: Dialectical synthesis
main_entities.context: Process of seeking higher-order truths by steel-manning different perspectives.

main_entities.name: Bretton Woods
main_entities.context: Post-World War II system of international cooperation that is now ending.

main_entities.name: Mutually Assured Destruction (MAD)
main_entities.context: Deterrent strategy based on the certainty of catastrophic retaliation in a nuclear war.

main_entities.name: Kardashev scale
main_entities.context: Method of measuring a civilization's level of technological advancement based on the amount of energy it can use.

main_entities.name: Grey Goo
main_entities.context: Hypothetical scenario involving self-replicating nanobots consuming all matter on Earth.

main_entities.name: Area 51
main_entities.context: A classified United States Air Force facility.

key_details.fact: The burning of Alexandria and the loss of the antikythera mechanism demonstrate that progress is not always steady.
The Drake equation may not be the end story on the likelihood of alien civilizations.
The tic tac UFO sighting is interesting and should not be immediately dismissed.
A scientist at Stanford analyzed material recovered from an Area 51-type area and found it to be a nanotech alloy.
The mimesis of desire is the fundamental generator of conflict.
We are the average of the five people we spend the most time around.
The individual affects the commons, and the collective conditions individuals.
The Bretton Woods solution is basically over or almost over.
Exponential tech and the decentralization of exponential tech means decentralized catastrophe weapon capacity.
